artifact.content_preview:
From: security.audit@tbs-sct.gc.ca
To: ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: itsec-director@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ciso@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: URGENT: ITSG-33 Compliance Gap — NGIS Phase 3 Network Migration
Date: Thu, 5 Mar 2026 08:47:15 -0500

Project Management Team,

During the scheduled ITSG-33 security compliance review for NGIS Phase 3
(review conducted February 28–March 4, 2026), our audit team identified
a Category 2 finding that must be addressed before the migration proceeds.

FINDING SUMMARY:
Control: IA

metadata.subject: URGENT: ITSG-33 Compliance Gap — NGIS Phase 3 Network Migration

risks.description: ITSG-33 Control IA-5(1) non-compliance: The SA&A package is missing Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) architecture documentation for privileged account management on target domain controllers, constituting a Category 2 finding that could block migration authorization.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The proposed Active Directory migration plan does not include documentation for privileged account management controls on the target domain controllers. Specifically, the Security Assessment and Autho

risks.description: March 18 migration window is at risk due to unresolved compliance finding. If SA&A documentation and PAW architecture review cannot be completed by March 12 and the CISO meeting resolved by March 9 week, the scheduled migration window will be missed.
affected_stakeholders: Downstream project phases dependent on Phase 3 completion

evidence_refs.excerpt: Until this finding is resolved, the March 18 migration window should be considered at risk.

risks.description: SSC's PBMM (Protected B, Medium Integrity, Medium Availability) cloud hosting authorization may be placed on hold, blocking cloud operations beyond the NGIS project itself.
affected_stakeholders: Other government departments using SSC-hosted cloud services

evidence_refs.excerpt: SSC's PBMM cloud hosting authorization may be placed on hold, potentially affecting other GC-Cloud workloads depending on the shared AD infrastructure.

risks.description: Cascading impact on other GC-Cloud workloads: Because the Active Directory infrastructure is shared, a hold on the PBMM authorization could disrupt unrelated government workloads that depend on the same AD infrastructure, creating a broad service availability risk.
affected_stakeholders: End users of GC cloud services

evidence_refs.excerpt: potentially affecting other GC-Cloud workloads depending on the shared AD infrastructure.

risks.description: Tight documentation deadline risk: The project team is required to deliver updated SA&A documentation addressing Control IA-5 by March 12, 2026 — only 6 calendar days from the email date of March 5 — creating a high risk of incomplete or inadequate documentation submission under time pressure.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation addressing Control IA-5 to this office no later than March 12, 2026.

risks.description: Resource availability risk: Scheduling a meeting between the SSC CISO office and the NGIS project team during the week of March 9 — while simultaneously preparing SA&A documentation due March 12 — places concurrent demands on key security and project personnel, risking resource contention and quality degradation.

evidence_refs.excerpt: A meeting between the SSC CISO office and the NGIS project team must be scheduled for the week of March 9 to review the PAW architecture.

risks.description: Implicit gap in project governance and quality assurance: The absence of PAW architecture documentation in the SA&A package suggests the project's internal security review process failed to catch this gap before the external TBS audit, indicating a systemic weakness in pre-audit compliance verification.

evidence_refs.excerpt: During the scheduled ITSG-33 security compliance review for NGIS Phase 3 (review conducted February 28–March 4, 2026), our audit team identified a Category 2 finding that must be addressed before the

risks.description: Budget risk from unplanned remediation work: Producing missing PAW architecture documentation, updating the SA&A package, and conducting emergency review meetings represents unplanned effort not likely budgeted in the original project plan, potentially causing cost overruns.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation addressing Control IA-5 to this office no later than March 12, 2026. A meeting between the SSC CISO office and the NGIS project team must b

risks.description: Implicit risk of incomplete PAW architecture in the actual technical design (not just documentation): The finding may indicate that PAW controls have not been fully designed or implemented — not merely undocumented — meaning the underlying security architecture itself may be deficient.
affected_stakeholders: Privileged users on target domain controllers

evidence_refs.excerpt: The proposed Active Directory migration plan does not include documentation for privileged account management controls on the target domain controllers.

risks.description: Reputational and relationship risk with TBS: Failure to respond with an action plan by end of day March 6 as explicitly requested could damage SSC's relationship with the Treasury Board Secretariat and signal poor project governance to the oversight body.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Please confirm receipt and provide an action plan by end of day March 6.

risks.description: Scope creep risk in Phase 3: The identification of a Category 2 finding late in the pre-migration review may trigger additional scrutiny of other controls in the SA&A package, potentially uncovering further compliance gaps that expand remediation scope beyond the current IA-5 finding.

evidence_refs.excerpt: During the scheduled ITSG-33 security compliance review for NGIS Phase 3 (review conducted February 28–March 4, 2026), our audit team identified a Category 2 finding.

risks.description: Privileged account security risk during migration window: Without confirmed PAW controls in place, any execution of the Active Directory migration exposes privileged accounts on target domain controllers to elevated risk of compromise during the transition period.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The proposed Active Directory migration plan does not include documentation for privileged account management controls on the target domain controllers.

parsed_json.description: ITSG-33 Control IA-5(1) non-compliance: The SA&A package is missing Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) architecture documentation for privileged account management on target domain controllers, constituting a Category 2 finding that could block migration authorization.

Specifically, the Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) package is missing the Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) architecture documentation required under ITSG-33 Annex 3A, Control IA-5(1).
